message:
perf(web/3d): optimize Model3DView camera tracking and WebGL renderer

## Problem

The 3D viewer had performance issues due to:
1. OrbitControls.update() returns void in Three.js, not a boolean indicating
   whether the controls changed - this caused unnecessary render cycles
2. WebGLRenderer was using alpha blending (slower) and default power preference
3. Stale closure issues with renderMode in effects due to direct state usage
4. Missing useEffect dependencies causing inconsistent behavior

## Solution

### Camera change detection via pose deltas
- Added `lastCameraPoseRef` tracking camera position (Vector3) and orientation
  (Quaternion) between frames
- Detect actual camera movement using `distanceToSquared()` and quaternion dot
  product with epsilon thresholds (1e-8)
- Only trigger re-renders when camera actually moves, not on every tick

### WebGLRenderer optimization
- Set `alpha: false` - disables alpha channel compositing (faster blending)
- Set `powerPreference: 'high-performance'` - requests discrete GPU on laptops
  with dual graphics (integrated + dedicated)

### Ref-based render mode tracking
- Added `renderModeRef` to track current render mode without closure capture
- Synced via useEffect to always have fresh value in async callbacks
- Fixed dependency arrays: removed `renderMode` (now ref-based), added
  `coarsePointer` and `requestRender` which were actually used

### State reset on model hide
- Reset `lastCameraPoseRef.initialized = false` when `show3DModel` becomes
  false, ensuring fresh pose tracking when model is shown again

## Technical details

Camera change detection math:
- Position delta: `pose.pos.distanceToSquared(camera.position) > 1e-8`
- Rotation delta: `1 - Math.abs(pose.quat.dot(camera.quaternion)) > 1e-8`
  (quaternion dot product = 1 when identical, using abs to handle q = -q)

These thresholds are small enough to catch sub-pixel movements but large
enough to filter floating-point noise from static cameras.

## Beads tracking

Updated issue comments for:
- phage_explorer-cuip: Ribosome traffic codon-rate heatmap scaling normalization
- phage_explorer-wzjl.1: Performance optimization implementation details

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"phage_explorer-cu3","content_hash":"52cb09e7f17178bd2efc312de05993c584b5d69abc9ea761272a11b69444ed6d","title":"Advanced: Metagenomic Co-Occurrence & Niche Profiler","description":"Infer ecological niches and co-occurring taxa from metagenomic abundance tables. SparCC/FlashWeave-like compositional correlations + NMF for latent niche vectors. Co-occurrence network colored by niches, habitat filters, significance thresholds. Adds real-world context to host predictions. Novelty: Medium-high, Pedagogical: High (compositional stats, niche ecology).","notes":"## METAGENOMIC CO-OCCURRENCE & ECOLOGICAL NICHE PROFILER

### Concept
Infer ecological niches and co-occurring taxa from metagenomic abundance tables; derive niche vectors and networks.

### How to Build
- **Input**: BIOM/TSV + sample metadata
- **Correlations**: SparCC/FlashWeave-like compositional inference in Rust+WASM (ndarray + rayon)
- **Niche factors**: NMF/PMF in TS for latent "niche vectors"
- **Metadata mapping**: Attach habitats/hosts to factors; cache per phage
- **UI**: Co-occurrence network colored by niches; stacked niche bars per phage; filters by habitat

### Why It's Good
Adds real-world context to host predictions; complements CRISPR/prophage evidence.

### Implementation Stack
Rust+WASM for correlations; TS NMF; SQLite cache of niche vectors; BIOM parser in TS.

### TUI Visualization
Network with niche colors; press 'n' to repaint bars; tooltips show top co-occurring taxa/habitats; toggle significance threshold live.

### Files
- packages/core/src/analysis/metagenomic-niche.ts

 {"id":"phage_explorer-d2p","content_hash":"3cf931099db9f603294bd3c5240ba226a6ca796952f92df60aded7885a1f22dc","title":"Advanced: Regulatory Signal Constellations","description":"Scan promoters/terminators/RBS/operators; render co-occurring motifs as constellations. PWMs + co-occurrence graphs. Evidence score combining spacing, orientation, strength into operon-likelihood. Starfield strip above genes; motifs as glyphs; toggle inferred operons. Novelty: Medium, Pedagogical: High.","notes":"## REGULATORY SIGNAL CONSTELLATIONS

**Full Implementation from Roadmap**

### Concept
Scan promoters/terminators/RBS/operators and render co-occurring regulatory motifs as 'constellations' to reveal operons and control logic.

### How to Build

**Motif Scans:**
- PWMs for sigma factors (σ70, σ32, σ54, σS)
- Rho-independent terminator detection (stem-loop + poly-U)
- RBS (Shine-Dalgarno) scoring
- Operator palindrome detection
- Run in TS (fast) or Rust+WASM for batch

**Co-occurrence Graph:**
- Build small graph of motifs per region
- Detect motif 'motifs' (regulatory patterns):
  - Promoter + operator + terminator spacing
  - Dual promoters (divergent)
  - Antitermination signals

**Evidence Score:**
- Combine spacing, orientation, and strength
- Operon-likelihood per gene cluster
- Regulatory complexity score

**Link to Genes:**
- Tie regulatory regions to downstream ORFs
- Annotate likely operons and regulons

### TUI Features
- Starfield strip above genes (motifs as glyphs)
- Edges show spacing relationships
- Hover shows PWM score for each motif
- Press 'o' to toggle inferred operon highlighting
- ORF blocks colored by regulatory context
- Legend showing motif glyph meanings

### Why It's Good
- Surfaces regulatory architecture beyond genes
- Shows how multiple motifs compose control logic
- Aids understanding of temporal expression
- Spacing and phase matter for function

### Implementation Stack
- TS PWM scanning
- Optional Rust+WASM for speed on large genomes
- SQLite motif hit cache
- Ink starfield rendering with braille and symbols

Novelty: 8/10, Pedagogical: 9/10, Wow Factor: 8/10","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T00:18:16.627038-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:48:55.796179-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-d2p","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-ojb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T00:20:54.703298-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
